framework.name: Multi-Agent System Architectures
framework.description: Three primary structural patterns for coordinating autonomous AI agents: decentralized networks (peer-to-peer with equal authority), hierarchical structures (tree-like with varying autonomy levels), and dynamic/distributed authority models. Each pattern trades off coordination overhead against flexibility and specialization depth.
framework.raw_quote: decentralized network...hierarchical structures which are treelike...authority doesn't have to be strictly top down or centralized. It can also be distributed across sub hierarchies
